summary: Phase 7 backend fully validated: 28/28 tests pass (test_phase7.py) — ws-ticket (RBAC + 404), WS handshake via single-use ticket (4401 on reuse + invalid), legacy ?token= still works, /webrtc/config (>=2 STUN, TURN opt-in), live-soap PUT/GET round-trip + provider-only RBAC, auto-draft from chat transcript, llm-soap via Claude Sonnet 4.5 (real API call, ~11s), recurrence weekly creates 4 + DELETE series cancels them, inventory lots increment stock + expiring window, treatment commission set/clamp/admin-only, /reports/commissions admin-only + shape, /push/public-key + subscribe (nested + flat) + unsubscribe + 400 on missing endpoint. Also caught and FIXED a critical Phase 7 regression: cancel_series wrote status='cancelled' (British) but AppointmentOut Literal accepts only 'canceled' (American) — every list endpoint started returning 500 ResponseValidationError after the recurrence test ran. Fixed in /app/backend/server.py:2759 + repaired 15 corrupt rows in db.appointments. Frontend Phase 7 UI testids verified in source (call-soap-toggle, call-soap-panel, soap-{S,O,A,P}, soap-{auto-draft,llm-draft,finalize}, appt-recur-{weekly,biweekly,monthly}, inv-lot-{id,number,qty,expiry,save}, expiring-banner, treatment-commission-{id}, comm-input-{id}, commission-save). Could not exercise every dialog visually because the seed DB has 0 inventory items, 0 treatments, and 0 future appointments at the moment of the run — but every backend path each dialog calls is covered by the 28 pytest cases. Routes confirmed: /portal/admin/inventory, /portal/admin/treatments (admin-only), /portal/provider/appointments, /portal/visit/:id.

minor.issue: DELETE /api/appointments/series/{id} wrote status='cancelled' which is NOT in the AppointmentOut Literal — every subsequent GET /api/appointments returned 500 ResponseValidationError. FIXED by tester (server.py:2759 + DB repair: 15 rows updated).

integration_issues.issue: Was showing 'Failed to load schedule' toast across the app due to the cancelled/canceled regression above. Root-caused and fixed.

action_items: Verify the cancelled→canceled fix in server.py:2759 stays in a future refactor (also consider migrating to the proper AppointmentStatus enum so this kind of typo is impossible).

Carry-over from iteration_3: NewAppointmentDialog still uses SelectItem value 'in_clinic' but AppointmentIn.visit_mode Literal expects 'in_person' / 'telehealth' — submitting 'In clinic' from UI will 422.
Carry-over: TelehealthVisit header reads appt.start_time instead of appt.start.

Phase 7 frontend dialogs (Lot, Commission, Recurrence) could not be exercised through the UI because seed DB had no rows; consider adding a small UI smoke seed (1 inventory item, 1 treatment, 1 future appointment) to make E2E testing reproducible.
Wire push notifications: infrastructure (subscribe/unsubscribe/public-key) is solid but no broadcast triggers exist anywhere — out of scope per request, just flagging.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG FIXED by tester (HIGH): /app/backend/server.py cancel_series() set status='cancelled' (British). AppointmentOut Pydantic Literal in /app/backend/models.py only accepts 'canceled' (American), so any GET /api/appointments that included a cancelled-series appointment 500'd with ResponseValidationError. Fix: change to 'canceled' + repair existing rows.

Recurrence endpoint uses naive integer day-deltas (weekly=7, biweekly=14, monthly=30). 'monthly' should walk to the same day-of-month, not +30 days — drifts by 2-3 days/year and breaks DST boundaries. Use dateutil.relativedelta(months=+1).

/visits/{id}/llm-soap regex fallback `r"\{.*\}"` returns the OUTERMOST braces; if Claude wraps JSON in code-fence prose, the parser can grab non-JSON. Consider `response.strip().removeprefix('```json').removeprefix('```').removesuffix('```')` first.

/push/subscribe accepts both nested {subscription:{...}} and flat shapes — good — but never validates `keys.p256dh`/`keys.auth` are present, so a malformed sub will quietly succeed and later fail when sending a push.
WS ticket TTL is 60s + ws_tickets collection has no TTL index. Will accumulate unbounded; add db.ws_tickets.create_index('expires_at', expireAfterSeconds=0) at startup.
server.py is now 2933 lines — refactor still deferred (carry-over).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_phase7.py (new, 28 tests, includes async WS via websockets lib)

success_rate.frontend: 100% testid presence verified in source; runtime UI dialogs not opened due to empty seed (0 inventory/treatments/future appts)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST P7 client + telehealth appointment + recurring weekly series (then cancelled) + TEST P7 Magnesium inventory item with LOT-P7-001 (qty 10, expires +30d) + TEST P7 Treatment (if absent) with 100% commission to practitioner. Push subscriptions for admin (test endpoints) created and cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 7 backend is fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_phase7.py (28 tests, mix of sync requests + async websockets). Run with: cd /app && export REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=$(grep REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL frontend/.env | cut -d= -f2) && export EMERGENT_LLM_KEY=$(grep EMERGENT_LLM_KEY backend/.env | cut -d= -f2) && pytest backend/tests/test_phase7.py --asyncio-mode=auto. NOTE: when running ALL phase{4,5,6,7} suites together the WS tests can intermittently timeout during handshake under load — running per-file is reliable. Phase 7 introduced one HIGH bug already fixed (cancelled->canceled spelling); watch for similar Literal mismatches in future PRs.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: /app/backend/server.py cancel_series() persisted status='cancelled' (British) into db.appointments. /app/backend/models.py AppointmentOut.status: Literal['requested','confirmed','completed','canceled','no_show'] — does NOT include 'cancelled'. FastAPI's response_model=List[AppointmentOut] then re-validated the GET /api/appointments output and raised ResponseValidationError -> 500 for every consumer (provider Appointments page, Up Next, calendar). Reproduction: POST /api/appointments/{id}/recurrence (count=4) -> DELETE /api/appointments/series/{series_id} -> GET /api/appointments returns 500. Mitigation: changed 'cancelled' to 'canceled' in server.py:2759 and ran a one-shot Mongo update_many to repair existing 15 polluted rows. All Phase 7 tests + appointment listing now green.
